bird.name: Bare-faced bulbul
bird.summary: The bare-faced bulbul (Nok hualon) is a member of the bulbul family of passerine birds. It was newly described from central Laos in 2009.

relations.subject: Bare-faced bulbul
relations.evidence: The bare-faced bulbul was assessed for the first time in 2010 by BirdLife International as of Least Concern, on the basis that the species has a large range, and there is insufficient evidence on population trend and size. In the paper describing the species it was suggested that it be listed as data deficient until further surveys of likely habitat were undertaken. The limestone karst in which it lives is threatened by quarrying and habitat degradation caused by overgrazing
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
